zh.lifeSummary: Margaret de Brampton是Brian de Brampton和Matilda St Valery的女兒，於大約1116年出生在英國戴文。她於1135年之前與Simon Corbet在英國史洛普爾朋特斯波利結婚。他們至少是9個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1185年在英國史洛普爾朋特斯波利享年70歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret de Brampton是Brian de Brampton和Matilda St Valery的女儿，于大约1116年出生在英国戴文。她于1135年之前与Simon Corbet在英国史洛普爾朋特斯波利结婚。他们至少是9个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1185年在英国史洛普爾朋特斯波利享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Brampton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from one or more of the many places called Brampton (Northants, Hunts, Herefs, Derbys, Lincs, WR Yorks, Norfolk, Suffolk, Westm, Cumb). There is a distinct early bias towards the E Midlands, E Anglia and Yorks.

variants.name: Brunton
meanings: Scottish and northern English: habitational name from Brunton in Fife, from Old English burna ‘stream’ + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. This surname is also common in Ireland.
English: variant of Brompton, a habitational name from any of the places called from Brompton in East and North Yorkshire, Middlesex, and Shropshire. Or, a variant of Branton 2.

variants.name: Brompton
meanings: 1  English: locative name from Brompton (ER Yorks, NR Yorks, Middx, Shrops).

variants.name: Branton
meanings: habitational name from any of the places called Branton in South Yorkshire (formerly in Yorkshire) and Northumberland or from Braunton in Devon. The first and last are named with Old English brōm ‘broom’ + tūn ‘farmstead, settlement’. The second is from an Old English word brēmen ‘overgrown with broom’ + tūn ‘farmstead’.

variant of Brampton, a habitational name from one or more of the many places called Brampton (Northamptonshire, Huntingtonshire, Herefordshire, Derbyshire, Lincolnshire, Yorkshire, Norfolk, Suffolk, Westmorland, and Cumberland), all of which are named with Old English brōm ‘broom’ + tūn ‘farmstead, settlement’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: Margaret de Brampton was born about 1116, in Devon, England as the daughter of Brian de Brampton and Matilda St Valery. She married Simon Corbet before 1135, in Pontesbury, Shropshire, England. They were the parents of at least 9 sons and 4 daughters. She died in 1185, in Pontesbury, Shropshire, England, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret de Brampton est née environ 1116, à Devon, Angleterre, fille de Brian de Brampton et Matilda St Valery. Elle a épousé Simon Corbet avant 1135, à Pontesbury, Shropshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 9 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée en 1185, à Pontesbury, Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret de Brampton wurde um 1116 in Devon, England, als Tochter von Brian de Brampton und Matilda St Valery geboren. Sie hat vor 1135 in Pontesbury, Shropshire, England Simon Corbet geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 9 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb 1185 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Pontesbury, Shropshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret de Brampton è nata il circa 1116 nel Devon, England come figlia di Brian de Brampton e Matilda St Valery. Lei sposato Simon Corbet prima del 1135, nel Pontesbury, Shropshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 9 figli e 4 figlie. Morì nel 1185, nel Pontesbury, Shropshire, England, all'età di 70 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Bramptonは約1116年にイギリスデボンでBrian de BramptonとMatilda St Valeryの娘として生まれた。1135年以前にイギリスシュロプシュアイアポンテスブリーでSimon Corbetと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも9人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1185年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアポンテスブリーで70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Brampton(은)는 대략 1116년 영국 데폰에서 Brian de Brampton(와)과 Matilda St Valery의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1135년 이전 영국 슈럽실르 폰츠버이에서 Simon Corbet(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 아홉 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1185년 영국 슈럽실르 폰츠버이에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret de Brampton nasceu aproximadamente 1116, em Devon, Inglaterra filha de Brian de Brampton e Matilda St Valery. Ela casou-se com Simon Corbet antes 1135, em Pontesbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 9 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1185, em Pontesbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret de Brampton родилась около 1116 г. в местности девон, Англия. Её родителей звали Brian de Brampton и Matilda St Valery. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Simon Corbet перед 1135 г. в местности понтесбури, шропшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 9 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла в 1185 г. в местности понтесбури, шропшир, Англия в возрасте 70 лет.
